market_context: US stock futures declined on March 12 as oil prices surged amid Iran war tensions, with WTI crude climbing 6% to $93/barrel and Brent reaching near $100/barrel[1]. Dow Jones futures fell 425 points (0.9%), S&P 500 futures declined 0.7%, and Nasdaq 100 futures traded lower[1]. The Energy Secretary announced a 172 million barrel Strategic Petroleum Reserve release, but market pressure persisted as geopolitical concerns outweighed supply relief measures[1].

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 futures down 0.7% at market open with oil-driven selling pressure[1]. Energy stocks showing strength (CF +9.16%, PATH +6.82%) but broad market weakness from elevated crude prices ($93 WTI) will likely persist through the session[1][5].

Dow Jones futures fell 425 points (0.9%) on March 12 morning with no significant positive catalysts to offset oil-driven headwinds[1]. Industrial and transportation sectors typically underperform during crude spikes, supporting downside bias.

Nasdaq 100 futures down 0.61% but tech showed relative resilience with Oracle (ORCL) up 9.18% on positive AI news[5]. Nasdaq's lower energy exposure and tech strength may allow modest recovery or outperformance versus broader indices by close[5].

Energy sector benefiting from 6% crude gains with WTI at $93 and Brent near $100 on Iran war supply concerns[1]. SPR release announcement insufficient to reverse uptrend; energy stocks (CF, PATH) already showing strong gains[5].
